title: read_pdf_file spawns pdftotext with no timeout, stderr leak, locale-dependent parser
description:
## Severity
Major

## Dimension
Security and Access / Error Orchestration / Perimeter Defense

## Location
src/tools/file/read.rs:90-174 (read_pdf_file)

## Violation
The function shells out to pdftotext and pdfinfo with no timeout, no resource limits, no stdout cap, and an information leak in stderr:

```rust
let mut cmd = Command::new("pdftotext");
if let Some(pages_str) = pages { ... }
cmd.arg(path);
cmd.arg("-");
match cmd.output() { ... }
```

`cmd.output()` blocks the calling thread until exit OR until the OS pipe buffer fills. A malformed PDF can pin a worker indefinitely. The worktree.rs module already implements git_with_timeout — read.rs ignores the lesson. Additional issues:

1. stderr returned verbatim into model context (line 160): `format!("pdftotext failed for '{path}': {stderr}")` — pdftotext error messages routinely contain absolute filesystem paths, enabling reconnaissance of jail layout.
2. pdfinfo stdout parsed with `line.starts_with("Pages:")` (line 121) — locale-sensitive. Under fr_FR pdfinfo emits "Pages :" and the 10-page guard is silently bypassed.
3. No total-byte cap on stdout; a 500-page PDF returns 5MB of text, exhausting token budget.

## Mandated refactor
1. Reuse the git_with_timeout pattern (extract a shared command::run_with_timeout helper — see separate DRY issue).
2. Cap pdftotext stdout to 100_000 chars matching read_text_file and signal truncation in the message.
3. Sanitize stderr: do not return upstream filesystem paths; log at tracing::warn! and return "pdftotext failed (check logs)".
4. Set LC_ALL=C in the spawn env so pdfinfo emits the C-locale field labels the parser expects.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
